summary: Lead accepts M4 muster preparation and mobilized force commitments after replacement writer R2 and systems_architect ACCEPT. The task adds M4 muster commitment commands, authoritative state, canonical validation/hash/query support, protocol parsing, and negative tests for response authority/provenance without expanding into downstream M4 march, supply, siege, battle, postwar, UI, save-format, or diplomacy scope.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-MUSTER-PREP-COMMITMENTS-001
tests_run.result: TASK_SHAPE_OK REVIEW systems_architect before lead ACCEPT state update.
Passed architecture boundaries, determinism, root Vitest 27 files / 180 tests, and web tests 1 file / 3 tests.
Passed format, lint, workspace typecheck, and architecture boundary checks.

tests_run.command: rg forbidden random/time/import scans over touched sim-core/protocol files
tests_run.result: No Math.random, Date.now, performance.now, new Date, React, Pixi, Electron, node:fs, or fs import matches.

decisions: No additional QA reviewer is required by this task file; systems_architect is the configured reviewer_role for this R3 simulation/protocol task.
Accepted the minimal validation-based authority split instead of a broader command schema split, preserving player/AI parity and public protocol scope.
Confirmed package.json only adds the new sim-core muster test file to the package test script and adds no dependency.
risks: M4 save-format persistence remains out of scope for this task and must be handled by the later M4 save/runtime task before save/load claims include muster commitments.
This task changes protocol and canonical world-state hash behavior, so PR CI and post-merge validation remain required before CLOSED.
requested_action: Commit accepted implementation, open PR, require CI, merge, sync main, close task only after integration.
